title: `Inspect pod` API endpoint result differs from API documentation
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

The inspect pod API endpoint returns JSON that's different than what's described in the documentation

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pod create --name test`

2. ``podman system service unix:///home/`whoami`/testing.sock --log-level=debug --time=500``

3. ``curl --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/pods/test/json``

**Describe the results you received:**
```json
{
   "Id":"6c3abdda50b5abb1f6a852ca6c9bda6b025754a2d84b46f275adc1892a86c18f",
   "Name":"test",
   "Created":"2021-10-01T10:13:12.150041866+02:00",
   "CreateCommand":[
      "./podman",
      "pod",
      "create",
      "--name",
      "test"
   ],
   "State":"Created",
   "Hostname":"test",
   "CreateCgroup":true,
   "CgroupParent":"/libpod_parent",
   "CgroupPath":"/libpod_parent/6c3abdda50b5abb1f6a852ca6c9bda6b025754a2d84b46f275adc1892a86c18f",
   "CreateInfra":true,
   "InfraContainerID":"980186f6414e5c0150b6da6d8cd9d1b7e5a44a2bf04bc1bc66613d9acfe4605c",
   "InfraConfig":{
      "PortBindings":{
         
      },
      "HostNetwork":true,
      "StaticIP":"",
      "StaticMAC":"",
      "NoManageResolvConf":false,
      "DNSServer":null,
      "DNSSearch":null,
      "DNSOption":null,
      "NoManageHosts":false,
      "HostAdd":null,
      "Networks":null,
      "NetworkOptions":null,
      "pid_ns":"private",
      "userns":"host"
   },
   "SharedNamespaces":[
      "ipc",
      "net",
      "uts"
   ],
   "NumContainers":1,
   "Containers":[
      {
         "Id":"980186f6414e5c0150b6da6d8cd9d1b7e5a44a2bf04bc1bc66613d9acfe4605c",
         "Name":"6c3abdda50b5-infra",
         "State":"configured"
      }
   ]
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
 JSON according to the documentation.
Missing fields:
```
cpu_period
cpu_quota
cpuset_cpus
devices
Labels
mounts
Namespace
```
Issues in `InspectPodInfraConfig`:
* Missing fields:
  ```
  cpu_period
  cpu_quota
  cpuset_cpus
  ```
* null instead of Array of strings:
  ```
  "DNSServer":null,
  "DNSSearch":null,
  "DNSOption":null,
  "HostAdd":null,
  "Networks":null,
  ```
* null instead of map<String, array of strings>
  ```
  "NetworkOptions":null,
   ```
* String instead of Array of integers <uint8> 
  ```
  "StaticIP":"",
  ```



**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Git Commit:   317e20a8d5a39088ea112f8321630aacfe27ddbc
Built:        Fri Oct  1 10:35:15 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, using self built latest Github dev version.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 10 WSL2, Debian Bullseye

comments.body: Empty fields are not displayed. If you do not specify any resource limit related data, they do not show up upon inspect. The same thing should happen when using podman locally.
Will open a PR to add this information to the swagger docs.
Is there currently any indication in the reference as to which fields exactly are `omitempty`?

I don't think this fixes this part:

> String instead of Array of integers
>`"StaticIP":"",`

Also, isn't `null` more bytes to transmit than `[]` in the case of an empty array?
